title: Discovery Gap / Search Space
description: A prescriptive description of where a missing mechanism would have to live to close one or more puzzles. Discovery gaps are the catalog's active face: they specify the shape of physics that should exist but doesn't (or hasn't been recorded), giving researchers a job description rather than a list of open questions. Each gap is updatable through three lifecycle stages: a mechanically scaffolded skeleton, an editorially drafted entry with literature pointers, and a reviewed entry whose closability has been assessed. When a new catalog mechanism closes the gap, the entry transitions to 'closed' with a pointer to the closing mechanism.

id.description: Stable slug used as the filename.

target_puzzles.description: Puzzle ids this gap is about. A single gap can target multiple puzzles when a single missing mechanism would close them together (e.g. an anthropic argument that fixes both the cosmological constant and Higgs mass).

status.description: draft_skeleton = mechanically scaffolded by scripts/scaffold_gaps.py from puzzle-horizon data, not yet hand-authored; drafted = editorial fields populated with literature pointers but closability not validated; reviewed = literature verified and closability_note written by hand; closed = a catalog mechanism now closes this gap (closed_by must be populated).

closability.description: plausible = existing literature aims at this with no fundamental obstruction; contested = some literature argues closable, others not; structurally_blocked = a no-go theorem or principle-level barrier exists; possibly_impossible = no published path, speculative; unknown = no editorial assessment yet (default for draft_skeleton).

closability_note.description: 1-3 sentence explanation of the closability label. Required by editorial convention for status >= drafted, but not enforced by the schema.

summary.description: Plain-English statement of what's missing, 1-2 sentences.

what_closes_it.description: Prose description of the shape a mechanism would need to have to close this gap. The most important editorial field: this is the job description for a future paper or catalog entry.

current_best_verdict_across_frameworks.description: Cached from scripts/search.py puzzle-horizon analysis. Indicates what the BEST any existing framework achieves on this gap's target puzzles. 'untouched' means no framework even references the puzzles; 'partial' means at most ameliorates.

required_field_content.description: Constraints on the spacetime-field content of a closing mechanism, expressed as set predicates over the field_tags vocabulary defined in schema/mechanism.schema.json. Empty arrays mean 'no constraint on this dimension'.

must_introduce_one_of.description: Closing mechanism must declare at least one of these field tags. Inferred or hand-curated from mechanisms that address related puzzles.

must_introduce_all_of.description: Closing mechanism must declare all of these field tags.

must_not_introduce.description: Field tags whose presence would conflict with the gap's logic (e.g. SUSY tags conflict with a non-SUSY hierarchy argument).

scale_hint.description: Free-text characterisation of the energy/length scale at which the closing mechanism is expected to operate. Examples: '10^16-10^19 GeV (UV-flow argument)', 'TeV scale (LHC-testable)', 'cosmological / IR'.

compatible_host_frameworks.description: Framework ids that could absorb a closing mechanism without exclusion-conflict. The set of frameworks where, if this gap is closed, the closing mechanism could be added to composes_mechanisms without breaking the framework's logic.

blocking_mechanisms.description: Mechanism ids that conflict with any reasonable gap-closer. If a host framework includes one of these, the closing mechanism cannot be added to that framework. (Distinct from compatible_host_frameworks: a framework may not host this gap-closer even if no listed blocking mechanism is in it, but if a listed blocking mechanism IS in it, the gap-closer cannot be added.)

principles_at_stake.description: Foundational principles a closing mechanism would have to respect, break, or test. Same enum as puzzle/anomaly schemas.

literature_anchors.description: Papers / reviews that aim at this gap or sketch a closing approach. Distinguished from puzzle references: these are about candidate routes to closing, not about the puzzle itself.

closed_by.description: Populated when status=closed. Points to the catalog mechanism that closed the gap and the framework in which the closure was first absorbed.
